heroSection.headline: Authentic, Career-Connected Learning
heroSection.subheadline: Connecting classroom learning with authentic workplace experiences to explore careers and develop employability skills.
heroSection.mission: To provide students with personalized, real-world learning opportunities to explore career pathways while building essential employability skills[cite: 1757, 1802].

programOverview.definition: Work-Based Learning (WBL) is a continuum of experiences that provide students with opportunities to practice and enhance skills gained in their Career and Technical Education (CTE) program of study through sustained interactions with industry professionals[cite: 221, 229].

forStudents: Develop transferable self-management and job-related skills[cite: 1735].
Apply classroom learning in authentic work settings[cite: 1737].
Gain insight into postsecondary and career decision-making[cite: 1739].
forPartners: Identify and train potential future employees early[cite: 1804].
Strengthen company presence in the community[cite: 1805].
Influence the next generation of skilled professionals[cite: 1806].
Gain fresh perspectives and energy on projects[cite: 1808].

wblContinuum.description: A progression of experiences ranging from awareness to career preparation[cite: 225].

stages.description: Activities designed to spark interest and gather information about career options.
Direct student contact with employers to gain knowledge of industry sectors.
Sustained interactions in real workplace settings aligned to curriculum.

studentCourseInfo.expectations: Students participate in both in-person instruction and supervised work-based training. [cite_start]They must log hours, complete reflections, and receive supervisor feedback[cite: 1758, 1777].

partnerEngagementMenu.description: Various ways local businesses can engage with BHS students[cite: 1807].

opportunities.benefit: Promote your company and industry to future talent[cite: 1808].
Showcase company culture; identify aptitude early[cite: 1808].
Access pipeline of skilled students; evaluate potential hires[cite: 1808, 1024].
Solve real business problems with student teams[cite: 1808].

bestPracticesForSupervisors: Emphasize safety and health training immediately[cite: 1296].
Provide a workplace orientation on the first day[cite: 1308].
Dedicate a mentor to guide the student and provide feedback[cite: 1303].
Explain workplace culture and expectations clearly[cite: 1300].

model1.skillsDeveloped: Lean manufacturing practices, equipment safety, quality control[cite: 1828].

model2.skillsDeveloped: Event planning, marketing branding, administrative skills[cite: 1909].

studentRequirements: Must be age 16+ for most Field Experience courses[cite: 1765].
Must adhere to safety protocols and confidentiality expectations[cite: 1925].
employerConsiderations: Child Labor Laws: Adhere to Illinois Child Labor Law of 2024 (820 ILCS 205)[cite: 1324].
Hazardous Occupations: Review restrictions for minors (e.g., power-driven machinery)[cite: 1326].
Insurance: Consult with insurance carriers regarding liability; students generally covered by student accident coverage[cite: 1340].
Training Agreements: A written training agreement/plan is required detailing tasks and responsibilities[cite: 1044].

links.title: US Dept of Labor Internship Fact Sheet
